# Baskets

> Single-tenant project management app (workspaces → projects → tasks → sub-tasks).
> This file documents the REST API so agents can drive Baskets programmatically.
> Base URL = this deployment's origin (e.g. http://localhost:5173). All API paths below are relative to it.

## Authentication

Every `/api/*` endpoint requires auth. Use a per-user API key as a bearer token:

```
Authorization: Bearer bsk_<token>
```

- The header must start literally with `Bearer bsk_`; anything else is treated as unauthenticated → `401`.
- Create keys in the web UI at `/settings` (API keys section). The plaintext key is shown exactly ONCE at creation; only its SHA-256 hash is stored. Format: `bsk_` + random token.
- A key inherits its owner's access. Banned users are rejected.
- The browser session cookie is also accepted (used by the web UI); agents should use a bearer key.

## Conventions

- Request bodies are JSON objects (`Content-Type: application/json`). A non-object body → `400 {"error":"Invalid JSON body"}`.
- All errors: `{ "error": "<message>" }` with the documented status code.
- `401 {"error":"Unauthorized"}` when auth is missing/invalid.
- Visibility: a project (or its tasks) the caller cannot access returns `404` — identical to a truly missing resource (never `403` on read), so inaccessible and nonexistent are indistinguishable.
- Timestamps in responses are ISO-8601 strings (e.g. `"2026-06-21T00:00:00.000Z"`); a nullable date is `null` when unset. Booleans are JSON `true`/`false`.
- Priority enum: `none | low | medium | high | urgent` (default `none`).
- Status category (drives behavior, not the name): `backlog | planned | in-progress | completed | canceled` (`completed` = the "done" bucket that completes sub-tasks; `backlog` = new-task default).

## Session

### GET /api/me
Identity of the current session/key holder.
- 200: `{ "user": { "id": string, "name": string } }`
- 401 if unauthenticated.

## Realtime

The web UI receives live updates and presence over a WebSocket at `/ws` (same origin), authenticated by the **browser session cookie** only — it is not part of the bearer-key REST surface. After any mutation through the API or UI, connected viewers of the affected project auto-refresh. Agents using a bearer key don't need to connect; just poll the REST endpoints. Messages are JSON: client→server `{type:'subscribe',projectId}` / `{type:'unsubscribe'}` / `{type:'ping'}`; server→client `{type:'changed',projectId}` (refetch) and `{type:'presence',projectId,users:[{id,name}]}`.

## Projects

### GET /api/projects
List accessible projects, newest first.
- 200: `{ "projects": [ <project>, ... ] }`

### POST /api/projects
Create a project.
- Body:
  - `name` string, required. Trimmed, non-empty, max 120.
  - `description` string|null, optional.
  - `workspaceId` string|null, optional. Must exist if given (`400 "unknown workspaceId"`); if omitted, defaults to the OLDEST workspace (or `400 "no workspace exists yet"` if none exist at all). Requires edit rights on the chosen workspace (`403 "No permission to create projects in this workspace"`).
- 201: `{ "project": <project> }`
- Errors: 400 (name/description/workspaceId), 401, 403.

### GET /api/projects/{id}
Fetch one project with nested resources.
- 200: `{ "project": <project>, "tasks": [<task>...], "views": [<view>...], "milestones": [<milestone>...], "locations": [<location>...], "statuses": [<status>...], "customFields": [<customField>...], "customFieldOptions": [<customFieldOption>...] }` (`statuses` = the project's eligible statuses; each task carries its decoded `customFields` map — see Object shapes).
- 404 if missing or inaccessible.

### PATCH /api/projects/{id}
Update project metadata. At least one field required (else `400 "No fields to update"`).
- Body: `name` (string, trimmed non-empty, max 120), `description` (string|null).
- `404` if missing OR inaccessible; `403 "No edit permission on this project"` only if accessible but the caller can't edit it.
- 200: `{ "project": <project> }`

### DELETE /api/projects/{id}
Delete a project (cascades to its tasks, views, milestones).
- `404` if missing or inaccessible; `403` only if accessible but not editable.
- 204: empty body.

## Tasks

### GET /api/tasks?projectId={id}
List a project's tasks (top-level + sub-tasks), ordered by position.
- `projectId` query param required (`400` if absent). `404` if the project is inaccessible/missing.
- 200: `{ "tasks": [<task>...] }`

### POST /api/tasks
Create a task or sub-task.
- Body:
  - `projectId` string, required.
  - `title` string, required. Trimmed, non-empty, max 240.
  - `priority` string, optional, default `none`. Must be in the priority enum.
  - `parentId` string|null, optional. If set, the parent must be a top-level task in the same project. Only one nesting level — sub-tasks cannot have children.
  - `statusId` string, optional — an eligible status id. Takes precedence over `status`.
  - `status` string, optional — an eligible status NAME (case-insensitive). Use this OR `statusId`.
    If neither is given, defaults to the first `backlog`-category status.
  - `description` string|null, optional.
  - `assigneeId` string|null, optional — must reference a valid user.
  - `milestoneId` string|null, optional — must reference a milestone of the same project.
  - `locationId` string|null, optional — must reference a saved location of the same project.
  - `dueDate` string|null, optional — `YYYY-MM-DD` (treated as midnight) or full ISO datetime.
  - `location` string|null, optional — free text `"lat, lng"` (plotted by map views).
  - `order` integer|null, optional — user rank for list views; null = unranked.
  - `customFields` object, optional — a `{ "<fieldId>": value }` map (see Custom fields). Each value is validated against its field's type; invalid → `400` (task not created).
- 201: `{ "task": <task> }` (the task includes its decoded `customFields` map).
- Any signed-in user with project access can create tasks. `404` if `projectId` is missing or inaccessible (indistinguishable, per ADR-019); `400` on validation errors.

### GET /api/tasks/{id}
Fetch one task and its sub-tasks.
- 200: `{ "task": <task>, "subTasks": [<task>...] }` (the task includes its decoded `customFields` map).
- `404` if missing or its project is inaccessible.

### PATCH /api/tasks/{id}
Update a task. At least one field (else `400 "No fields to update"`).
- Body: any of `title` (non-empty, max 240), `statusId` or `status` (name), `priority`, `description`, `assigneeId` (string|null), `milestoneId` (string|null, same project), `locationId` (string|null, same project), `location` (string|null, legacy text), `order` (integer|null), `dueDate` (string|null), `parentId` (string|null — re-parent this task), `customFields` (`{ "<fieldId>": value }` map; see Custom fields — an empty/null value clears the field).
- `parentId`: move the task under another top-level task of the SAME project (makes it a sub-task), or `null`/`""` to detach it back to top-level. Rejected (`400`) if the target is itself a sub-task (only one nesting level), if it's the task itself, or if this task already has sub-tasks of its own.
- Done-cascade: setting a parent to a `completed`-category status cascades that status to all its sub-tasks.
- 200: `{ "task": <task> }`
- `404` if missing or its project is inaccessible; `403 "No edit permission on this task"` if accessible but the caller can't edit it.

### DELETE /api/tasks/{id}
Delete a task and its sub-tasks.
- `404` if missing or inaccessible; `403` only if accessible but not editable.
- 204: empty body.

## Custom fields

Projects define custom fields (created/edited in the project settings UI — no field-definition REST write endpoints). Each task stores a value per field. Field definitions + their select options are returned by `GET /api/projects/{id}`; task values appear as the per-task `customFields` map on task responses and are written via the `customFields` map on POST/PATCH `/api/tasks`.

Value encoding in the `customFields` map (key = field id):
- `text` / `email` / `phone` / `url`: string. (email/phone/url are format-validated.)
- `number`: number (or numeric string). Stored canonically; rendered per the field's number format.
- `checkbox`: boolean.
- `date`: ISO string (`YYYY-MM-DD`, or full ISO datetime when the field shows time).
- `select`: array of option ids (single-select = 1-element array). Option ids must belong to the field.
- `person`: array of user ids.
- `place`: array of `location` ids (same project).
- `task`: array of task ids (same project). A single-link field (no `config.multi`) keeps just the first id; with `config.multi` it links several. A legacy bare-id string is still accepted and read back as a 1-element array.
- `files`: array of `file` ids (upload first via `/api/files`).
- An empty array / null / "" clears the value.

Type is immutable after a field is created. There is no sort/group/filter by custom fields via the API (display only).

## Files

Files for `files`-type custom fields are stored on the server's local disk and served only through these endpoints.

### POST /api/files
Upload a file (multipart/form-data, NOT JSON).
- Form parts: `fieldId` (required, a `files`-type field), `file` (required), `taskId` (optional — must belong to the field's project, else `400`). The project is derived from the field.
- Limits: max 10 MB (`413`); executable + active-markup extensions (svg, html, xml, js, exe, …) are rejected (`415`). The stored `mimeType` is **server-derived from the extension** (the client-claimed MIME is ignored).
- `400` if no `file` part is provided, the file is empty (0 bytes), or `fieldId` is missing / does not reference a `files`-type field.
- 201: `{ "file": { "id": string, "filename": string, "mimeType": string, "size": number } }`. Add the returned `id` to the task's `files` field value (PATCH `/api/tasks/{id}` with `customFields`).
- `404` if the field's project is inaccessible.

### GET /api/files/{id}
Download the file bytes (`404` if missing, inaccessible, or the bytes are no longer on disk). Sent with `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` + `Content-Security-Policy: sandbox`; raster images stream `inline`, everything else `attachment`.

### DELETE /api/files/{id}
Delete a file (row + disk bytes) and strip its id from any task value referencing it. `200: { "success": true }` on delete; `404` if missing/inaccessible.

## Task attachments

Files can also be attached DIRECTLY to a task (no custom field). These rows have `taskId` set and `fieldId` null, and back the task gallery / cover image.

### GET /api/tasks/{id}/files
List the files attached directly to a task (excludes custom-field files).
- 200: `{ "files": [{ "id", "taskId", "fieldId", "filename", "mimeType", "size" }...] }`. `401` if unauthenticated; `404` if the task or its project is inaccessible.

### POST /api/tasks/{id}/files
Upload a file straight onto a task (multipart/form-data; part `file`). The project is derived from the task; max 10 MB (`413`); blocked executable/active-markup extensions rejected (`415`); same server-derived MIME as `/api/files`.
- 201: `{ "file": { "id", "taskId", "fieldId" (null), "filename", "mimeType", "size" } }`. `400` if no `file` part or the file is empty; `401` if unauthenticated; `404` if the task is missing/inaccessible OR the caller lacks edit permission (this endpoint returns `404` in all those cases, never `403`).
- Set a task's cover image from one of these via the `?/setTaskCover` form action (`id`, `coverFileId`; image of the same task; empty clears).

## Comments

### GET /api/tasks/{id}/comments
List a task's comments plus its activity feed (newest-relevant order).
- 200: `{ "comments": [{ "id", "taskId", "authorId", "authorName", "body", "createdAt", "updatedAt", "edited" }...], "activity": [{ "id", "projectId", "taskId" (nullable), "actorId", "actorName", "type", "data", "createdAt" }...] }`. `401` if unauthenticated; `404` if inaccessible.

### POST /api/tasks/{id}/comments
Add a comment.
- Body (JSON): `{ "body": string }` (non-empty, max 10000).
- 201: `{ "comment": <comment> }`. `400 "body is required"` if missing/empty, `400 "body too long (max 10000)"` if over 10000; `404` if inaccessible.

### PATCH /api/comments/{id}
Edit a comment body (author or admin only).
- Body (JSON): `{ "body": string }` (non-empty, max 10000).
- 200: `{ "comment": <comment> }`. `400` if the body is invalid JSON, empty, or over 10000 chars; `403` if not the author/admin, `404` if missing.

### DELETE /api/comments/{id}
Delete a comment (author or admin only). `403`/`404` as above. 204: empty body.

## Notifications

In-app notifications for the current user (assignment + due-date reminders). Due reminders are generated lazily on GET.

### GET /api/notifications
List the current user's notifications + unread count.
- 200: `{ "notifications": [{ "id", "userId", "type", "body", "projectId", "taskId", "read" (boolean), "createdAt" }...], "unreadCount": number }`. Returns at most the **50 most-recent** notifications (newest first); `unreadCount` counts ALL unread, not just the returned page. `type` ∈ `assigned | due_soon | overdue | ...`.

### POST /api/notifications/{id}
(also `PATCH`) Mark one notification read (owner-scoped). 200 on success, `404` if not the user's.

### POST /api/notifications/read-all
(also `PATCH`) Mark all of the user's notifications read. 200: `{ "ok": true, "count": number }` (`count` = number marked read). `401` if unauthenticated.

## Templates

Task templates: capture a task (and its sub-tasks + custom values) for reuse.

### GET /api/projects/{id}/templates
List templates available to a project (its own + its workspace scope).
- 200: `{ "templates": [{ "id", "name", "scope", "projectId", "workspaceId", "payload" (JSON string), "createdBy", "createdAt" }...] }`. `401` if unauthenticated; `404` if inaccessible.

### POST /api/projects/{id}/templates
Create a template, OR instantiate one into the project (the body shape selects the path).
- **Create** body: `{ "name": string (trimmed, non-empty, max 120), "payload": { "task": { "title": string (required), "description"?: string|null, "priority"?, "recurrence"?: string|null, "fields"?: { "<fieldId>": string|null } }, "subtasks"?: [ <same task shape> ] }, "scope"?: "project" (default) | "workspace" }` → `201 { "id": string }`. Needs project structure-edit rights; a `scope:"workspace"` template ALSO needs workspace-edit rights — otherwise `404`. `400` on `name` missing/too long or `payload.task.title` missing.
- **Instantiate** body: `{ "templateId": string }` (or `{ "instantiate": true, "templateId": ... }`) → `201 { "taskId": string }` (the new top-level task id); creates the task + its sub-tasks + custom values. Only needs project access. `400` if `templateId` is missing or the template can't be instantiated.
- `400` on invalid JSON body; `401` if unauthenticated; `404` if the project is inaccessible.

## Export

### GET /api/projects/{id}/export?format=csv
Download all project tasks as CSV (UTF-8 BOM, CRLF, RFC-4180 quoting). Columns: Title, Parent, Status, Priority, Assignee, Milestone, Due Date, one per custom field. Access-gated (`404` otherwise); `400 "Unsupported format (only csv)"` if `format` is anything but `csv` (case-insensitive); served as a `Content-Disposition: attachment` download.

## Object shapes

- project: `id, name, description, workspaceId, statusId, icon, statusDisplay, pinned, startDate, dueDate, createdBy, createdAt, updatedAt` (`icon` = emoji or `iconoir:<name>` (nullable); `statusDisplay` ∈ `text|icon|text-icon`; `startDate`/`dueDate` nullable ISO date strings)
- task: `id, projectId, parentId, title, description, statusId, priority, assigneeId, milestoneId, locationId, location, order, position, startDate, dueDate, recurrence, coverFileId, createdBy, createdAt, updatedAt` (`startDate`/`dueDate` nullable ISO dates; `recurrence` = repeat-rule string|null; `coverFileId` = a task-attachment file id|null), plus `customFields` (a `{ "<fieldId>": decodedValue }` map on API task responses). `locationId` → a saved project `location` (below; settable via POST/PATCH /api/tasks); `location` is the legacy freeform `"lat, lng"` text (still accepted; map views prefer `locationId`).
- location: `id, projectId, title, address, latitude, longitude, position, createdAt, updatedAt` — project-scoped saved places (`title` required; `address`/`latitude`/`longitude` nullable). Plotted by map views only when both lat+lng are set. Listed under `GET /api/projects/{id}`; reference one from a task via `locationId`. The records are created/edited/deleted in the project settings UI (no location write REST endpoints).
- status: `id, name, description, category, color, icon, projectId, workspaceId, position, builtIn, createdAt`. Built-in ids → category: `status-backlog` (backlog), `status-planned` (planned), `status-in-progress` (in-progress), `status-completed` (completed), `status-canceled` (canceled).
- customField: `id, projectId, entity, name, type, config (JSON object), appliesTo, position, createdAt`. `entity` ∈ `task | project` (project-entity fields ARE returned in `GET /api/projects/{id}` `customFields`, but their values live on the project, not tasks). `type` ∈ `text|number|select|date|person|files|task|checkbox|email|phone|place|url|rollup` (`rollup` is computed client-side and never stored, so it never appears in a task's `customFields` map). `appliesTo` ∈ `all | tasks (top-level only) | subtasks` — a value can only be set on a task at the matching level (else `400`). `config` is type-specific: number `{numberFormat, currencyCode?, formatString?}`; select `{multi, displayOption}`; date `{dateFormat, timeFormat}`; person/place/files/task `{multi}`; rollup `{relation, targetFieldId, formula}`.
- customFieldOption: `id, fieldId, title, color, icon, position, createdAt` — members of a `select` field.
- file: `id, projectId, fieldId, taskId, filename, mimeType, size, createdBy, createdAt` (the on-disk path is never exposed; fetch bytes via `GET /api/files/{id}`).
- view: `id, projectId, name, type (table|board|list|timeline|calendar|dashboard|map), config (JSON string), position, isDefault, hidden, createdBy, createdAt, updatedAt`. `config` is schemaless per-view JSON; known **table/list** keys: `groupBy` (`status|milestone|assignee|due|label`; omit = ungrouped), `sortBy` (`priority|order|title|due|status|assignee|createdAt`, each optionally `:desc`; omit = natural order), `filters` (each array lists the **checked (shown)** values for that facet: `{ statusIds?, priorities?, assigneeIds?, milestoneIds?, labelIds?, dueBuckets? }`. A facet filters only when its key is **present**: absent = inactive = all shown; a present array shows only those values; an empty present array shows nothing; `_none` shows unassigned / no-milestone / no-label / no-due; a task is shown if ANY of its labels is checked), column toggles `priority|assignee|milestone|due|labels` and per-custom-field `cf:<fieldId>` (boolean; absent = shown; table only), `colWidths` (table only — `{ "<colKey>": px }`, colKey ∈ `status|title|priority|assignee|milestone|due|labels|cf:<fieldId>|actions`; presence switches the table to fixed-width columns), `statusIds` (array of status ids to show; absent = all). **board** key: `groupBy` (`status` default `|milestone|assignee|label` — non-status values become swimlanes). `display` (`text|text-icon|icon`) applies to any view's tab.
- milestone: `id, projectId, name, description, startDate, targetDate, position, createdAt, updatedAt` (`startDate`/`targetDate` nullable ISO dates). Returned under `GET /api/projects/{id}` and assignable via a task's `milestoneId`; created/edited/deleted in the UI only (no milestone write REST endpoints).

## Example

```
curl -X POST https://YOUR_HOST/api/tasks \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer bsk_xxx" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"projectId":"<id>","title":"Write the spec","priority":"high","status":"In progress"}'
```
